#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Add observer relationship
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // 4. Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 5. Return notification status
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);

    // Get the singleton graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer.get(), target.get());

    // Notify the target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Record call count before reset
    int initialCount = observer->getCallCount();

    // Reset the observer
    observer->reset();

    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != initialCount) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }

    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create source and target nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName + "_source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName + "_target");

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: target observes source
    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Notify source - should trigger target
    source->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify target was notified
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset target state
    target->valueChanged(false);

    // Close the target node
    target->close();

    // Notify source again - target should not be notified
    source->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify target was not notified after closure
    if (target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    
    // Create first calculation (depends on var_a and var_b)
    auto calc1 = reaction::calc([&]() { 
        return var_a.get() + var_b.get(); 
    });
    
    // Create second calculation (depends on calc1)
    auto calc2 = reaction::calc([&]() { 
        return calc1.get() * 2; 
    });
    
    // Update the first variable
    var_a.set(newA);
    
    // Return the final result of the chain
    return calc2.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create named reactive variables
    reaction::Var<int> varA("varA", valueA);
    reaction::Var<int> varB("varB", valueB);
    
    // Create named calculation
    auto calcSum = reaction::Calc<int>("calcSum", 
        [&varA, &varB]() { return varA.get() + varB.get(); },
        varA, varB);
    
    // Create named action
    auto actionMul = reaction::Action("actionMul",
        [&varA, &varB]() {
            int result = varA.get() * varB.get();
            // Action execution logic (result can be used as needed)
        });
    
    // Retrieve names and combine into a single string
    std::string result = varA.getName() + "," + 
                        varB.getName() + "," + 
                        calcSum.getName() + "," + 
                        actionMul.getName();
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <cmath>
#include <limits>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive sensor variable
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Track recomputation count and last processed value
    auto recompute_count = reaction::var(0);
    auto last_value = reaction::var(std::numeric_limits<double>::quiet_NaN());
    
    // Step 3: Create filter for significant changes
    auto change_filter = reaction::filter::change(diffThreshold);
    auto filtered_sensor = sensor.filter(change_filter);
    
    // Step 4: Create calculation that depends on filtered sensor
    auto calc = reaction::calc([filtered_sensor, &recompute_count, &last_value, levelThreshold]() {
        double current_val = filtered_sensor.get();
        
        // Update last value and recompute count
        if (std::isnan(last_value.get())) {
            last_value.set(current_val);
        }
        recompute_count.set(recompute_count.get() + 1);
        
        // Determine status
        std::string status = (current_val > levelThreshold) ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
        return status;
    });
    
    // Step 5: Update sensor with first value
    sensor.set(firstUpdate);
    
    // Step 6: Record recompute count after first update
    int count_after_first = recompute_count.get();
    
    // Step 7: Update sensor with second value
    sensor.set(secondUpdate);
    
    // Step 8: Record recompute count after second update
    int count_after_second = recompute_count.get();
    
    // Step 9: Get final status
    std::string final_status = calc.get();
    
    // Step 10: Return results
    return {final_status, count_after_first, count_after_second};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {

    // Get updated expression value
    int updatedExpressionValue = expr();
    
    // Get squared value after update
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate = calc();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        initialExpressionValue,
        updatedExpressionValue,
        squaredValueAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();
    
    // Register the main thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // Capture initial state
    std::size_t threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Create and start worker thread
    std::thread workerThread([&threadManager]() {
        threadManager.registerThread();
        // Simulate some work
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));
    });
    
    // Wait for worker thread to complete
    workerThread.join();
    
    // Capture final state
    std::size_t threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore, 
        safetyAfter, 
        threadCountBefore, 
        threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    auto calculation = reaction::calc(
        [&](int x) {
            recomputeCount++;
            return x + deltaA + deltaB;
        },
        reactiveVar
    );
    
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeCount;
    
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        reactiveVar.set(reactiveVar.get() + deltaA);
        reactiveVar.set(reactiveVar.get() + deltaB);
    });
    
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeCount;
    int finalValue = calculation.get();
    
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        recomputeCountBeforeBatch,
        recomputeCountAfterBatch,
        finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    auto var = reaction::var(initialValue);
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() { 
        recomputeCount++;
        return var(); 
    });
    
    reaction::Batch batch;
    
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    batch.add([&]() { var = updatedValue; });
    batch.execute();
    
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    batch.close();
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        closedInitially,
        closedAfterClose,
        recomputeCountAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        // Implementation for valueChanged
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    CacheProbeNode nodeA, nodeB, nodeC;
    
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    graph.registerNode(&nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(&nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(&nodeC);
    
    // Direct observer: nodeA observes nodeB
    nodeB.addOneObserver(&nodeA);
    
    // Transitive observer: nodeB observes nodeC
    nodeC.addOneObserver(&nodeB);
    
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.getObservers(&nodeA, observers);
    
    graph.populateCache();
    
    auto statsBefore = graph.getStats();
    
    graph.cleanupCache();
    
    auto statsAfter = graph.getStats();
    
    nodeA.close();
    nodeB.close();
    nodeC.close();
    
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        statsBefore.graphEntries,
        statsAfter.graphEntries,
        statsAfter.hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create a constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = reaction::constVar(constValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that sums both variables
    auto sum = reaction::calc([](int m, int c) { return m + c; }, mutableVar, constVar);
    
    // Get the initial sum
    int initialSum = sum.get();
    
    // Update the mutable reactive variable
    mutableVar.set(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get the updated sum
    int updatedSum = sum.get();
    
    // Return the result
    return {initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();
    
    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadSafetyStatus();
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getActiveThreadCount();
    
    std::thread worker([&]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard guard;
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));
    });
    
    worker.join();
    
    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadSafetyStatus();
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getActiveThreadCount();
    
    return ThreadGuardState{
        safetyBeforeGuard,
        safetyAfterGuard,
        threadCountBeforeGuard,
        threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    auto sourceVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    auto calculation = reaction::calc(
        [var = &sourceVar]() { return var->get(); },
        reaction::LastHandle{}
    );
    
    int initialResult = calculation.get();
    sourceVar->set(updatedValue);
    int updatedResult = calculation.get();
    sourceVar->close();
    int closedResult = calculation.get();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = closedResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>

    uint64_t thirdValue;
    bool strictlyIncreasing;
};

UniqueIdSequence test_unique_id_sequence() {

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitor::disable();
    StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    StackMonitorReport report;
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = StackMonitor::isEnabled();
    
    {
        StackGuard guard(1024);
        report.guardAllocated = guard.isAllocated();
        report.usageDuringGuard = StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
        
        auto stats = StackMonitor::getStatistics();
        report.usagePercentage = (static_cast<double>(stats.used) / stats.total) * 100.0;
        report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.near_limit;
    }
    
    report.usageAfterGuard = StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
    StackMonitor::disable();
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = StackMonitor::isEnabled();
    
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Lambda to create large payload with seed-based initialization
    auto makeLargePayload = [](int seed) -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = seed + static_cast<int>(i);
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Create small SBOResource and update its value
    reaction::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    SmallPayload updatedPayload{updatedSmall};
    bool updateReportedChange = (smallResource.update(updatedPayload) != 
                                reaction::UpdateResult::UNCHANGED);

    // Create large SBOResource
    LargePayload largePayload = makeLargePayload(largeSeed);
    reaction::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource(largePayload);

    // Retrieve storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();

    return SboStorageStatus{
        updateReportedChange,
        smallInfo.isUsingSBO(),
        smallInfo.stackUsage(),
        largeInfo.isInitialized(),
        largeInfo.isUsingSBO()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;
};

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    tm.reset();
    
    // 3. Create a reactive variable
    auto sourceVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 4. Create a calculation
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&sourceVar, multiplier]() {
        return sourceVar.get() * multiplier;
    });
    
    // 5. Get value before reset
    int beforeResetValue = calc.get();
    
    // 6. Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety(true);
    
    // 7. Check if thread safety is enabled
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset calculation logic
    calc.reset();
    
    // 9. Update source variable
    sourceVar.set(initialValue + 1);
    
    // 10. Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calc.get();
    
    // 11. Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        beforeResetValue,
        afterResetValue,
        safetyEnabledDuringReset
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,

    int line,

    int pointerValueAfterWrite;
    bool pointerStable;

    int pointerValueAfterWrite = *ptr2;
    
    // Check if the pointer is stable (same address)
    bool pointerStable = (ptr1 == ptr2);
    
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initialPointerValue,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = pointerValueAfterWrite,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with the provided names
    auto var1 = React::var(firstName);
    auto var2 = React::var(secondName);
    auto var3 = React::var(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into a set to order them using React::operator<
    std::set<React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names from the set
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& var : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(var.name());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique by comparing the size of the set of names
    std::set<std::string> uniqueNames(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end());
    bool allUnique = (uniqueNames.size() == 3);
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackLimitSnapshot {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBeforeGuard;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfterGuard;
    bool templateAllocationAllowed;
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed;
    bool guardHeld;
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld;
};

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    // Enable stack monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::enable();
    
    // Initialize the stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Get stack usage statistics before creating the guard
    auto statsBeforeGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getUsageStats();
    
    // Check if template-based stack allocations are allowed
    bool templateAllocationAllowed = 
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isTemplateAllocationAllowed();
    
    // Check if runtime stack allocations are allowed
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed = 
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isRuntimeAllocationAllowed();
    
    // Create a large StackGuard instance to reserve significant stack space
    constexpr size_t LARGE_STACK_SIZE = REACTION_STACK_SIZE_LIMIT;
    reaction::memory::StackGuard guard(LARGE_STACK_SIZE);
    
    // Check if the guard was successfully held
    bool guardHeld = guard.isHeld();
    
    // Check if stack allocation is blocked while the guard is held
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = false;
    if (guardHeld) {
        // Attempt to allocate a small chunk of stack memory
        // This should fail if the guard is properly blocking allocations
        size_t smallAllocationSize = 1024; // Small size to test blocking
        allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = 
            !reaction::memory::StackMonitor::tryAllocate(smallAllocationSize);
    }
    
    // Get stack usage statistics after the guard is created
    auto statsAfterGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getUsageStats();
    
    // Return the comprehensive stack limit snapshot
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBeforeGuard,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfterGuard,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllocationAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllocationAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardHeld,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

related functions.description: Force cleanup of expired or excess cache entries

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

class IntCache : public reaction::CacheBase<int, int> {
public:

    using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::lookup;
    using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
    using reaction::CacheBase<int, int>::invalidateAll;

    // Step 8: Get cache statistics after insertion

    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = postInsertStats.entries;
    
    // Step 9: Invalidate all cache entries
    cache->invalidateAll();
    
    // Step 10: Trigger cleanup
    cache->cleanup();
    
    // Step 11: Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto postInvalidationStats = cache->getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = postInvalidationStats.entries;
    
    // Calculate hit ratio
    std::size_t hitCount = postInvalidationStats.hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount = postInvalidationStats.missCount;
    double hitRatio = (hitCount + missCount) > 0 ? 
                      static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount) : 0.0;
    
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        entriesBefore,
        entriesAfterInsert,
        entriesAfterInvalidate,
        hitCount,
        missCount,
        hitRatio,
        firstLookupWasMiss,
        secondLookupWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
